title: [agent-fix] Add SFL Loop monitoring to Organizations tree
body:
Closes #128

Adds read-only SFL Loop monitoring under each SFL-enabled repository in the Organizations sidebar tree so users can see pipeline health without opening the repo detail view first.

## Changes

- **`src/types/sflStatus.ts`** — New shared types for SFL workflow info, overall status enum (`Healthy`, `Active work`, `Blocked`, `Ready for review`, `Recent failure`), status derivation logic, and display constants
- **`src/api/github.ts`** — New `fetchSFLStatus()` method on `GitHubClient` that detects SFL workflows via the Actions API and fetches latest run status for each
- **`src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts`** — SFL state management (`sflStatusData`, `loadingSFLStatus`, `expandedSFLGroups`), caching through `dataCache`, and fetch/toggle callbacks
- **`src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/OrgRepoTree.tsx`** — New collapsible `SFL Loop` tree node under each repo showing overall status badge, summary row, and per-workflow rows with enabled/disabled state and latest run conclusion
- **`src/components/sidebar/GitHubSidebar.tsx`** — Passes new SFL props through to `OrgRepoTree`

## Acceptance Criteria (from issue)

- ✅ Repos with core SFL workflows show an `SFL Loop` child node in the Organizations tree
- ✅ Repos without SFL workflows do not show the node (hidden after detection)
- ✅ Read-only v1 — no workflow dispatch, rerun, or label mutation actions
- ✅ Overall SFL summary represents: Healthy, Active work, Blocked, Ready for review, Recent failure
- ✅ Expanded SFL node shows workflow children with enabled/disabled state and latest run status
- ✅ Status data cached via existing `dataCache` flow — no ad hoc polling
- ✅ Works for all detected SFL-enabled repos via workflow name detection, not hardcoded list

Risk Acknowledgment: `risk:medium` — adds new status aggregation and tree rendering logic, but is strictly read-only and does not mutate GitHub state.




> Generated by [SFL Issue Processor / Implementer](https://github.com/relias-engineering/hs-buddy/actions/runs/22804324617) for issue #128

[MARKER:sfl-analyzer-a cycle:0]
## 📊 SFL Analysis A — Full-Spectrum Review

**Analyzer**: A
**Cycle**: 0
**PR**: #129
**Linked Issue**: #128

### Blocking Issues 🔴

> Issues that MUST be fixed before this PR can merge.

- [ ] **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:71-88]** — `deriveSFLOverallStatus` never returns `'ready-for-review'`. The PR marks the acceptance criterion "Overall SFL summary represents: Healthy, Active work, Blocked, Ready for review, Recent failure" as ✅, but the derivation logic has no branch that produces `'ready-for-review'`. The checks proceed: `recent-failure` → `active-work` → `blocked` (all disabled) → `healthy` (all success/no-runs) → `unknown`. There is no path that inspects GitHub issue labels, PR states, or any other signal to derive `'ready-for-review'`. The status type, label, icon, and display constant all exist, but the value is unreachable in practice. Either the derivation logic must be extended (e.g., by checking for open PRs awaiting review among the repo's data), or the acceptance criterion and PR checklist must be corrected to reflect that `ready-for-review` is deferred to a future iteration.

### Non-Blocking Suggestions 🟡

> Improvements that would be nice but are not required for merge.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:80-85]** — When all SFL workflows are `active` but none have ever run (`latestRun === null` for all), `deriveSFLOverallStatus` returns `'healthy'` because `allSuccess = workflows.every(w => !w.latestRun || ...)` is `true`. A freshly-configured SFL repo with no workflow history will display as "Healthy". Consider treating this case as `'unknown'` by changing the condition to require at least one successful run: e.g., `const allSuccess = workflows.some(w => w.latestRun) && workflows.every(...)`.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:499]** — `fetchedSFLRef.current.add(key)` is called before the async fetch, and is never cleared on failure. If `fetchSFLStatus` throws (network error, permission denied), `fetchedSFLRef` still has the key, so toggling the node closed and re-expanding never retries. Users are stuck seeing no data unless they restart the app. Consider removing the key from `fetchedSFLRef` in the error handler, or only adding it on success.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:457-518]** — SFL status does not participate in the sidebar's `refreshTick` cycle. Once fetched, `fetchedSFLRef` blocks re-fetch forever, so SFL data goes stale and is never refreshed during an active session. The existing `refreshInterval` mechanism could clear `fetchedSFLRef` (or just the relevant keys) to trigger a background refresh on the next expand.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:56-64]** — `SFL_STATUS_COLORS` is exported but never imported or used in any file touched by this PR. This is dead code. Either use it for CSS class generation in `sflOverallStatusIcon`/`sflWorkflowStateIcon`, or remove it.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/OrgRepoTree.tsx:313-390]** — The SFL Loop node rendering uses an inline IIFE (`(() => { ... })()`), which is harder to follow than a named component. Extracting this to a `SFLLoopNode` component would improve readability, enable isolated testing, and remove the three-level nesting of conditional logic.

- **[src/api/github.ts:1826]** — `listRepoWorkflows` is called with `per_page: 100` but does not paginate. A repo with more than 100 workflows would silently miss SFL workflows on page 2+. Extremely unlikely in practice, but worth a comment acknowledging the limitation or a guard like `if (workflowsResponse.data.total_count > 100) console.warn(...)`.

- **[general]** — No tests were added for `deriveSFLOverallStatus` (unit-testable pure function), `fetchSFLStatus` (mockable API call), or the SFL tree rendering logic. Issue #128 implementation step 8 calls this out explicitly. Even a few snapshot/unit tests for the status derivation edge cases would prevent regressions.

### Verdict

**BLOCKING ISSUES FOUND** | 1 blocking issue, 7 non-blocking suggestions.

> Generated by [SFL Analyzer A — Full-Spectrum Review](https://github.com/relias-engineering/hs-buddy/actions/runs/22804502700) for issue #129

[MARKER:sfl-analyzer-b cycle:0]
## 📊 SFL Analysis B — Full-Spectrum Review

**Analyzer**: B
**Cycle**: 0
**PR**: #129
**Linked Issue**: #128

### Blocking Issues 🔴

> Issues that MUST be fixed before this PR can merge.

- [ ] **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:66-88]** — `deriveSFLOverallStatus` has no code path that returns `'ready-for-review'`. The acceptance criterion states "Overall SFL summary represents: Healthy, Active work, Blocked, Ready for review, Recent failure", and the PR marks this as ✅. However, the function checks: failure → active-work → blocked (all disabled) → healthy (all success/no-runs) → unknown. The `'ready-for-review'` status is defined in the type, has a label, icon, and color constant, but is unreachable. Either extend the derivation logic to detect this state (e.g., by checking for open PRs awaiting human review), or update the PR and issue acceptance criteria to note that `ready-for-review` is deferred to a future iteration.

### Non-Blocking Suggestions 🟡

> Improvements that would be nice but are not required for merge.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:82-85]** — When all SFL workflows are `active` but none have ever run (`latestRun === null` for all), the function returns `'healthy'` because `allSuccess = workflows.every(w => !w.latestRun || ...)` is true when all have null runs. A freshly-configured SFL repo with zero workflow history will show "Healthy" when it should arguably show "Unknown" or "No data yet". Consider requiring at least one successful run: `const allSuccess = workflows.some(w => w.latestRun) && workflows.every(...)`.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:508-510]** — `fetchedSFLRef.current.add(key)` is called before the async fetch completes, and is never cleared on failure. If `fetchSFLStatus` throws (network error, permission denied, rate limit), `fetchedSFLRef` still contains the key, so collapsing and re-expanding the node never retries the fetch. Users would need to restart the app to retry. Consider removing the key from `fetchedSFLRef` in the error handler, or only adding it on success.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts]** — SFL status does not participate in the sidebar's `refreshTick` cycle. Once fetched, `fetchedSFLRef` blocks re-fetch forever, so SFL data goes stale during long sessions. The existing `refreshInterval` mechanism clears `fetchedRepoPRsRef` but not `fetchedSFLRef`. Consider including SFL data in the refresh cycle to keep status current.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:54-61]** — `SFL_STATUS_COLORS` is exported but never imported or used anywhere in this PR. This is dead code. Either use it for CSS class generation in `sflOverallStatusIcon`/`sflWorkflowStateIcon` (replacing the hardcoded class names), or remove it to keep the module clean.

- **[src/api/github.ts:1826]** — `listRepoWorkflows` is called with `per_page: 100` but does not handle pagination. A repository with more than 100 workflows would silently miss SFL workflows on page 2+. While extremely rare, consider adding a warning or comment acknowledging the limitation.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/OrgRepoTree.tsx:313-390]** — The SFL Loop node uses an inline IIFE (`(() => { ... })()`), which adds three levels of nesting and is harder to follow than a named component. Extracting this to a `SFLLoopNode` component would improve readability and enable isolated testing.

- **[general]** — No tests were added for `deriveSFLOverallStatus` (a pure function that's easy to unit test), `fetchSFLStatus` (mockable API call), or the SFL tree rendering. Issue #128 mentions tests in implementation step 8. Even minimal unit tests for the status derivation edge cases would prevent regressions.

### Correctness & Logic ✅

- The workflow detection using `SFL_CORE_WORKFLOW_FRAGMENTS` correctly identifies SFL workflows via case-insensitive substring matching.
- The `fetchSFLStatus` method correctly fetches workflow inventory and latest run status in parallel using `Promise.all`.
- The `deriveSFLOverallStatus` function correctly prioritizes failure → active → blocked → healthy, with the noted exception of `ready-for-review`.
- The tree rendering correctly handles the loading state, empty state, and populated state.
- Edge case: The chevron click handler on line 338 has `e.stopPropagation()` followed by the same `onToggleSFLGroup` call as the parent — this is intentional to prevent double-toggling when clicking directly on the chevron.

### Security ✅

- No secrets, credentials, or tokens are exposed or logged.
- The feature is read-only — no mutation operations are exposed (no workflow dispatch, rerun, or label changes).
- All data flows through the existing authenticated `GitHubClient` with proper token management.
- No XSS vectors — all rendered content is structured data, not user-provided HTML.

### Performance ✅

- Workflow run fetches use `per_page: 1` to minimize payload size — good practice.
- Fetches are parallelized with `Promise.all` to minimize latency.
- Data is cached via `dataCache` to prevent redundant API calls.
- The fetch queue uses `priority: -1` to deprioritize SFL fetches relative to more critical data.
- Potential concern: Expanding many repos simultaneously could trigger many parallel API requests, but the queue management should handle this.

### Style & Maintainability ✅

- Import ordering is consistent with the existing file style.
- Type definitions are clean and well-documented with JSDoc comments.
- The `SFL_CORE_WORKFLOW_FRAGMENTS` constant is appropriately placed in the types file with the related interfaces.
- CSS class naming follows the existing `sidebar-*` and `sfl-*` conventions.

### Verdict

**BLOCKING ISSUES FOUND** | 1 blocking issue (unreachable `ready-for-review` status), 7 non-blocking suggestions.

> Generated by [SFL Analyzer B — Full-Spectrum Review](https://github.com/relias-engineering/hs-buddy/actions/runs/22804606116) for issue #129

[MARKER:sfl-analyzer-c cycle:0]
## 📊 SFL Analysis C — Full-Spectrum Review

**Analyzer**: C
**Cycle**: 0
**PR**: #129
**Linked Issue**: #128

### Blocking Issues 🔴

> Issues that MUST be fixed before this PR can merge.

- [ ] **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:66-88]** — `deriveSFLOverallStatus` cannot produce `ready-for-review`, but the PR claims that state is represented and marks that acceptance criterion as complete. The function only returns `recent-failure`, `active-work`, `blocked`, `healthy`, or `unknown`; therefore one required summary state is unreachable, so acceptance criteria are not fully met.
- [ ] **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:460-530]** — SFL status is cached but never refreshed by the existing sidebar refresh flow. There is refresh logic for repo PR tree and repo counts, but no equivalent refresh path for `sfl-status:*`, which violates the acceptance criterion requiring status to be cached **and refreshed** through the existing sidebar data flow.

### Non-Blocking Suggestions 🟡

> Improvements that would be nice but are not required for merge.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:498-510]** — `fetchedSFLRef.current.add(key)` is set before fetch completion and never rolled back on failure, so transient failures can prevent retry for the session.
- **[src/api/github.ts:1843-1865]** — The per-workflow `catch` swallows errors and returns `latestRun: null` for both “no runs” and “request failed” cases; consider surfacing an explicit error flag to avoid masking API failures as neutral state.
- **[general]** — No tests were added for `deriveSFLOverallStatus` or SFL sidebar behavior, despite issue step 8 explicitly requesting focused tests.

### Verdict

**BLOCKING ISSUES FOUND** | 2 blocking issue(s), 3 non-blocking suggestion(s).

[MARKER:sfl-analyzer-a cycle:1]
## 📊 SFL Analysis A — Full-Spectrum Review

**Analyzer**: A
**Cycle**: 1
**PR**: #129
**Linked Issue**: #128

### Summary of Cycle 1 Changes Reviewed

The Issue Processor addressed both blocking findings from cycle 0:

1. **`ready-for-review` unreachable path (Analyzers A + B + C)** — Fixed by adding a `hasActionRequired` check in `deriveSFLOverallStatus` that returns `'ready-for-review'` when any workflow has `conclusion === 'action_required'`.
2. **SFL status not participating in refresh cycle (Analyzer C)** — Fixed by adding a new `useEffect` that iterates `fetchedSFLRef.current` on the existing `refreshInterval`, calling `fetchSFLStatusForRepo(org, repoName, true)` to bypass cache on stale entries, and adding an `isRefresh` parameter to `fetchSFLStatusForRepo` matching the existing `fetchRepoPRsForRepo` pattern.

### Blocking Issues 🔴

> Issues that MUST be fixed before this PR can merge.

_None found._

### Non-Blocking Suggestions 🟡

> Improvements that would be nice but are not required for merge.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:71-73]** — The `action_required` conclusion is used as a proxy for `ready-for-review`. In GitHub Actions, `action_required` means a workflow is awaiting environment protection rule approval (deployment gates), not that an SFL-generated PR is awaiting human code review. In practice, SFL repos are unlikely to use environment protection rules on their core workflows, so this signal may almost never fire naturally. The derived state is now reachable as required (fixing the blocking issue), but a comment explaining the approximation — or a note in the PR description that the `ready-for-review` state is a best-effort approximation from Actions conclusions — would clarify intent for future maintainers. Low urgency for v1.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:80-85]** — `deriveSFLOverallStatus` still returns `'healthy'` when all active SFL workflows have `latestRun === null` (no run history). `workflows.every(w => !w.latestRun || ...)` evaluates to `true` when all `latestRun` are `null`. A freshly configured SFL repo would show "Healthy" instead of "Unknown." Carried over from cycle 0 — still present, low urgency.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:56-64]** — `SFL_STATUS_COLORS` is exported but never imported or used in any file in this PR. Dead code; flagged in cycle 0, remains unaddressed. Remove or use it.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:509]** — `fetchedSFLRef.current.add(key)` is called in `toggleSFLGroup` before the async fetch completes and is never rolled back on failure. A transient network error silently blocks retry for the entire session (the refresh interval provides eventual recovery, but the user has no way to force an immediate retry without restarting the app). Flagged in cycle 0, remains. Low impact given the periodic refresh now exists.

- **[src/api/github.ts:1826]** — `listRepoWorkflows` uses `per_page: 100` with no pagination. A repository with more than 100 workflows silently misses page-2+ results. Extremely unlikely in practice; at minimum a `console.warn` if `total_count > 100` would surface the limitation. Carried over from cycle 0.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/OrgRepoTree.tsx:313-390]** — The SFL Loop node rendering remains an inline IIFE. Extracting to a named `SFLLoopNode` component would improve readability and testability. Carried over from cycle 0, cosmetic.

- **[general]** — No unit tests were added for `deriveSFLOverallStatus` or SFL tree rendering behavior. Implementation step 8 in issue #128 explicitly calls for focused tests. The pure `deriveSFLOverallStatus` function is especially straightforward to unit-test and would catch regressions like the cycle 0 unreachable-state bug automatically.

### Correctness & Logic ✅

- The `hasActionRequired` check correctly reads from `w.latestRun?.conclusion`, which is populated only when `status === 'completed'`. It cannot conflict with the `hasRunning` check (which reads `status === 'in_progress'/'queued'`). Priority ordering is correct: failure → running → action_required → blocked → healthy → unknown.
- The `isRefresh` parameter correctly bypasses the `dataCache.get` guard without affecting `fetchedSFLRef`, so the refresh loop can call `fetchSFLStatusForRepo` repeatedly without the "first-fetch-only" guard blocking it.
- The refresh `useEffect` iterates `fetchedSFLRef.current` (repos ever expanded) and skips entries whose cache is still fresh — preventing redundant API calls on each tick. Repo name splitting handles names with slashes (`org/team/repo` → `key.split('/')` → `[org, ...repoParts]` → `repoParts.join('/')`). ✅
- The `loadingSFLStatus` spinner fires during background refresh, which gives appropriate visual feedback.

### Security ✅

- No new secrets, tokens, or credentials introduced.
- Feature remains strictly read-only; cycle 1 changes add no mutation surfaces.
- The refresh path creates a `GitHubClient` with existing `accounts` context — consistent with the rest of the hook.

### Performance ✅

- Background refresh shares the existing `refreshInterval` timer — no new polling infrastructure.
- `dataCache.isFresh` guard prevents redundant fetches on each interval tick.
- Expanding many repos concurrently is gated by the existing `enqueueRef` task queue with `priority: -1`.

### Style & Maintainability ✅

- `isRefresh = false` default parameter follows the same signature as `fetchRepoPRsForRepo`. Consistent with project patterns.
- New `useEffect` mirrors the structure of the existing repo-PR refresh effect verbatim. Easy to review and maintain.

### Verdict

**PASS** | No blocking issues found. Both cycle 0 blocking issues have been correctly addressed. 7 non-blocking suggestions (6 carried over from cycle 0, 1 new note on the `action_required` semantic approximation).

[MARKER:sfl-analyzer-b cycle:1]
## 📊 SFL Analysis B — Full-Spectrum Review

**Analyzer**: B
**Cycle**: 1
**PR**: #129
**Linked Issue**: #128

### Summary of Cycle 1 Changes Reviewed

The Issue Processor addressed both blocking issues from cycle 0:

1. **`ready-for-review` unreachable path** — Fixed by adding `hasActionRequired` check in `deriveSFLOverallStatus` that returns `'ready-for-review'` when any workflow has `conclusion === 'action_required'`.
2. **SFL status not participating in refresh cycle** — Fixed by adding a new `useEffect` with `setInterval` that iterates `fetchedSFLRef.current` and calls `fetchSFLStatusForRepo(org, repoName, true)` when cache is stale.

### Blocking Issues 🔴

> Issues that MUST be fixed before this PR can merge.

_None found._

### Non-Blocking Suggestions 🟡

> Improvements that would be nice but are not required for merge.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:71-73]** — The `action_required` conclusion is used as a proxy for `'ready-for-review'`. In GitHub Actions, `action_required` specifically means a workflow is awaiting environment protection rule approval (deployment gates), not that an SFL-generated PR is awaiting human code review. This state may rarely fire in practice for SFL repos unless they use deployment environments with required reviewers. A code comment explaining this approximation would help future maintainers understand the mapping. Low urgency for v1 read-only feature.

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:80-85]** — `deriveSFLOverallStatus` returns `'healthy'` when all active SFL workflows have `latestRun === null` (no run history). The condition `workflows.every(w => !w.latestRun || w.latestRun.conclusion === 'success' || ...)` evaluates to `true` when all `latestRun` are `null`. A freshly-configured SFL repo with zero workflow history will display as "Healthy" when it arguably should show "Unknown" or "No data yet". Consider: `const hasAnyRun = workflows.some(w => w.latestRun); if (!hasAnyRun) return 'unknown';`

- **[src/types/sflStatus.ts:54-61]** — `SFL_STATUS_COLORS` is exported but never imported or used anywhere in this PR. Dead code. Either use it in `sflOverallStatusIcon`/`sflWorkflowStateIcon` to dynamically apply CSS classes, or remove it to keep the module clean.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/useGitHubSidebarData.ts:509]** — `fetchedSFLRef.current.add(key)` is called in `toggleSFLGroup` before the async fetch completes and is never rolled back on failure. A transient network error or rate limit silently blocks retry until the next refresh interval or app restart. Consider removing the key in the error handler, or only adding it on success, to allow immediate retry on user re-expand.

- **[src/api/github.ts:1826]** — `listRepoWorkflows` fetches with `per_page: 100` but does not handle pagination. A repository with more than 100 workflows would silently miss SFL workflows on subsequent pages. While extremely rare in practice, adding a `console.warn` if `total_count > 100` would surface the limitation during development/debugging.

- **[src/components/sidebar/github-sidebar/OrgRepoTree.tsx:313-390]** — The SFL Loop node rendering uses an inline IIFE (`(() => { ... })()`), adding three levels of nesting and making the code harder to follow. Extracting to a named `SFLLoopNode` component would improve readability and enable isolated unit testing. Low urgency, cosmetic.

- **[general]** — No unit tests were added for `deriveSFLOverallStatus` (a pure function that's straightforward to test) or SFL tree rendering behavior. Issue #128 implementation step 8 explicitly calls for focused tests. Even minimal tests for the status derivation edge cases (all failures, mixed states, no runs, all disabled) would catch regressions automatically.

### Correctness & Logic ✅

- The `hasActionRequired` check correctly reads `w.latestRun?.conclusion` which is only populated when `status === 'completed'`. It cannot conflict with `hasRunning` (which checks `status === 'in_progress'/'queued'`).
- Priority ordering is correct: failure → running → action_required → blocked (all disabled) → healthy (all success/no-runs) → unknown.
- The `isRefresh` parameter correctly bypasses the `dataCache.get` guard without affecting `fetchedSFLRef`, enabling the refresh interval to re-fetch without being blocked by the "first-fetch-only" guard.
- The refresh `useEffect` correctly iterates `fetchedSFLRef.current` and skips entries whose cache is still fresh via `dataCache.isFresh()`.
- Repo name splitting handles names with slashes correctly (`key.split('/') → [org, ...repoParts] → repoParts.join('/')`).
- The loading spinner (`loadingSFLStatus`) correctly fires during background refresh, providing visual feedback.

### Security ✅

- No secrets, tokens, or credentials are exposed or logged.
- The feature remains strictly read-only — no workflow dispatch, rerun, or label mutation operations are exposed.
- All data flows through the existing authenticated `GitHubClient` with proper token management.
- No XSS vectors — all rendered content is structured data, not user-provided HTML.

### Performance ✅

- Background refresh shares the existing `refreshInterval` timer — no new polling infrastructure.
- `dataCache.isFresh()` guard prevents redundant fetches on each interval tick.
- Workflow run fetches use `per_page: 1` to minimize payload size.
- Fetches are parallelized with `Promise.all` to minimize latency.
- The fetch queue uses `priority: -1` to deprioritize SFL fetches relative to more critical data.
- Expanding many repos concurrently is gated by the existing `enqueueRef` task queue.

### Style & Maintainability ✅

- `isRefresh = false` default parameter matches the existing `fetchRepoPRsForRepo` signature — consistent with project patterns.
- New `useEffect` for refresh mirrors the structure of the existing repo-PR refresh effect — easy to review and maintain.
- Import ordering is consistent with existing file style.
